voice_line: A 6th-round pick who became the greatest to ever do it.

games.notes: NFL debut. Mop-up duty in Thanksgiving blowout
Bledsoe injured. Brady enters in relief

moments.description: Drew Bledsoe goes down. A 6th-round pick nobody has heard of trots onto the field. He never gives the job back.
moments.voice_line: Nobody knew. He never gave it back.

games.notes: AFCCG. Bledsoe came in for injured Brady, then Brady returned

moments.description: The Rams were 14-point favorites. The Greatest Show on Turf. Brady drove the Pats into field goal range with 1:21 left. Vinatieri kicked it through. Dynasty born.
moments.voice_line: Youngest QB to win a Super Bowl. The impossible beginning.

games.notes: Super Bowl XXXVI MVP. Youngest SB-winning QB
Super Bowl XXXVIII MVP. Second ring
Super Bowl XXXIX. Third ring in four years
Wait this seems wrong for a loss. Let me fix
AFC Championship. Manning comeback. Colts went on to win SB

moments.description: 18-0. One game from perfection. David Tyree pins a ball against his helmet with Rodney Harrison draped on him. The perfect season dies on one play.
First quarter. Week 1. Bernard Pollard rolls into Brady's left knee. ACL torn. The 2008 season — his age-31 prime — just vanishes.
moments.voice_line: Gone in the first quarter of the first game.

games.injury: ACL tear - left knee. Season over
games.notes: ACL torn in first quarter. Season over
The return. Monday Night. Comeback win
Wild card. Ray Rice went off. Brady's worst playoff game
65-play drive to end it by BUF
AFC Championship. Lee Evans drop. Billy Cundiff miss
Super Bowl XLVI. Manningham sideline catch. Wes Welker drop
AFC Championship loss. Ravens went on to win SB
OT loss. Wes Welker in Denver
AFC Championship loss. Manning went to SB
Monday Night. 41-14. Is Brady done? Trent Dilfer says so
Rested starters late. Had #1 seed locked

moments.description: After a 45-7 AFC Championship demolition of the Colts, the NFL investigates ball pressure. What follows is a two-year legal saga, a 4-game suspension, and the most scrutinized footballs in history.
moments.voice_line: 45-7 and somehow they were the story.

moments.description: Down 10 in the third quarter. Brady rallies the Patriots. Then on the goal line with 20 seconds left — Seattle passes. Malcolm Butler jumps the route. Interception. Fourth ring.
moments.voice_line: They should have run it. They didn't.

games.notes: Super Bowl XLIX. Malcolm Butler pick. Fourth ring
AFC Championship. Von Miller dominated. Last game before Deflategate suspension
Return from suspension. 406 yards. Statement game
Only loss as starter this season

moments.description: Down 28-3 with 17:28 left in the third quarter. No team had ever come back from more than 10 points in a Super Bowl. Brady threw for 466 yards. The Patriots scored 31 unanswered. Won in overtime. The single greatest performance in the history of team sports.
moments.voice_line: Down 28-3 with 17 minutes left. Won anyway.

games.notes: Super Bowl LI. 28-3 comeback. OT. 5th ring. SB MVP

moments.description: 505 passing yards. 3 TDs. The greatest statistical Super Bowl performance by a quarterback. And he lost. Nick Foles caught a touchdown. Brady's Hail Mary fell through his fingers.
moments.voice_line: 505 yards. 3 touchdowns. And it wasn't enough.

games.notes: Super Bowl LII. Philly Special. 505 yards in a loss
AFC Championship. OT at Arrowhead. Rex Burkhead TD
Super Bowl LIII. 13-3. Ugly but dominant. Sixth ring
Wild card. Derrick Henry ran wild. Brady's last game as a Patriot
First home win as a Buc
Thursday. Lost fumble in final minute
Divisional. Beat Brees in his last game
NFC Championship. 3 picks but still won

moments.description: First year in Tampa Bay. Everyone said he was done. Beat Patrick Mahomes — the heir apparent — in the Super Bowl. At his home stadium. Seventh championship. Most in history by any player in any sport.
moments.voice_line: They said he was done. Seven rings.

games.notes: Super Bowl LV. Seventh ring. Home stadium
Divisional. Down 27-3, almost came back. Final game? Then un-retired
Season finale. 8-9. Won NFC South anyway
Wild card. Last game of career. 9-8 season. Retired Feb 1

milestones.label: 6th Round, Pick 199
milestones.description: Selected by New England Patriots. Four QBs taken before him. The 199th player chosen.

milestones.label: First Career Start
milestones.description: Replaced injured Drew Bledsoe. Started against Indianapolis Colts. Never gave the job back.

milestones.label: Super Bowl XXXVI
milestones.description: Beat the St. Louis Rams 20-17. Super Bowl MVP. Youngest QB to win a Super Bowl at 24.

milestones.label: Super Bowl XXXVIII
milestones.description: Beat the Carolina Panthers 32-29. Second Super Bowl MVP.

milestones.label: Super Bowl XXXIX
milestones.description: Beat the Philadelphia Eagles 24-21. Three rings in four years.

milestones.label: Unanimous MVP
milestones.description: 50 TDs. 16-0. First unanimous MVP in NFL history. Set single-season TD record.

milestones.label: ACL Tear — Season Lost
milestones.description: Torn ACL in Week 1. Missed entire 2008 season. 16 games of void.

milestones.label: Super Bowl XLIX
milestones.description: Beat the Seattle Seahawks 28-24. Malcolm Butler interception. Fourth ring.

milestones.label: Deflategate Suspension
milestones.description: 4-game suspension. Came back and won the Super Bowl.

milestones.label: Super Bowl LI
milestones.description: Beat the Atlanta Falcons 34-28 OT. 28-3 comeback. Fifth ring. Fourth SB MVP.

milestones.label: Super Bowl LII Loss
milestones.description: Lost to Philadelphia Eagles 33-41. 505 passing yards in a loss.

milestones.label: Super Bowl LIII
milestones.description: Beat the Los Angeles Rams 13-3. Sixth ring. Most by any player.

milestones.label: Signed with Tampa Bay
milestones.description: Left New England after 20 seasons. Free agent signing. The divorce.

milestones.label: Super Bowl LV
milestones.description: Beat the Kansas City Chiefs 31-9. Seventh ring. First year in Tampa. Home stadium.

milestones.label: Retirement
milestones.description: Retired after 23 NFL seasons. 7 Super Bowls. 89,214 passing yards. 649 TDs. The GOAT.
